title: podman wait generates high CPU load
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

> Uncomment only one, leave it on its own line:
>
/kind bug
>
> /kind feature

**Description**

The podman processes are consuming quite a bit of CPU, but the jobs are actually completing successfully. They are just running incredibly slowly. It appears that the slowdown is coming from cpu-intensive podman wait commands.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. sudo podman run -d --cgroup-parent='/hadoop-yarn/' docker-registry:4443/hadoop/docker_configs/rhel6 sleep 600

02f2ec419309bcf16d32f3a87483bad52d44da665984c6e7307e492b54b0d3f5

2. sudo podman wait 02f2ec419309bcf16d32f3a87483bad52d44da665984c6e7307e492b54b0d3f5 &

**Describe the results you received:**

podman wait generates high CPU load.

```
for i in {1..10}; do ps -ao %cpu,%mem,cmd; sleep 1; done
%CPU %MEM CMD

 0.0  0.0 ps -ao %cpu,%mem,cmd
%CPU %MEM CMD

 0.0  0.0 ps -ao %cpu,%mem,cmd
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

podman wait shouldn't generate so much load on the CPU

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

100% reproducible

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ sudo podman version
Version:       0.8.5-dev
Go Version:    go1.9.4
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
$ sudo podman info

  Conmon:

    path: /usr/libexec/crio/conmon

  GraphDriverName: overlay
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Physical system running RHEL 7.5

comments.body: This one looks simple - the we're using PollImmediateInfinite() to wait for the container to exit, which accepts a duration parameter for how long to wait before testing again. This duration is hard-coded to 1 right now, which should be 1 nanosecond. I think we probably want to stretch that out - 100 millisecond poll intervals should be fine, I think?
Opened #1392 to increase the interval between checks
